title: NOC 64321 – Casino workers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 64321 (Casino workers) includes the skilled dealers and guest service representatives who power Canada's regulated gaming industry. Operating everything from high-stakes poker tables to modern slot machine systems, these TEER 4 professionals ensure a fair and entertaining environment for patrons. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the newly launched 2026 TR to PR Pathway, which prioritizes workers already contributing to the hospitality and entertainment sectors within Canada.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6432 – Tourism and amusement services occupationsNOC Code64321

duties: Operate gaming tables for games such as roulette, blackjack, poker, and baccarat.
Monitor and assist patrons using slot machines and perform minor adjustments or repairs.
Explain the rules of games to patrons and ensure they are followed.
Calculate and pay out winning bets and jackpots, and collect losing bets.
Maintain related reports and replenish slot machines.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Security Clearance: Mandatory for all casino workers in Canada.
Gaming License: A provincial casino gaming license is usually required for all employees.
On-the-job training is provided for gaming table dealers.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. Employment growth will lead to a few new positions, while a moderate number of openings will arise due to retirements. The industry remains resilient as it integrates online and physical gaming experiences.
labour_market_demand: The demand for casino workers is Moderate to Good, with high concentrations in Ontario (Toronto and Niagara Falls) and British Columbia (Greater Vancouver). As new resort-casinos open and existing facilities modernize their gaming floors, the need for licensed table dealers and slot technicians is increasing.
Regulated Industry: Work in a highly secure, professional environment with strict labor protections.
Clear Career Path: Progression to Casino Supervisor (NOC 62022) is common after gaining experience.
Stable Full-Time Work: Unlike many tourism roles, casino positions are often full-time and year-round with comprehensive health benefits.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure to transition 33,000 temporary workers into permanent residents. Hospitality and service sectors are expected to be high priority.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A dedicated stream for those working in tourism and hospitality for at least 9 months.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): For workers in Nova Scotia, NB, or PEI with a qualifying job offer from a designated employer.
